programmes.show_description: Nathan Shepherd plays the best Indie Love Songs by Warpaint, The Maccabees and The 1975!
5/10. The Birth of the Liverpool Sound. Various interviews with and about the Beatles covering the period from 1960 - Spring 1961. Presenter: Brian Matthew.
1/10. Getting It On Wax. Various interviews with and about the Beatles covering the period Summer 1961 - Summer 1962. Refs. to the Cavern club in Liverpool. Presenter: Brian Matthew.
4/4. 6 Music Festival 2026 headliner Courtney Barnett shares her songwriting inspirations.
Want to hear the latest tracks 6 Music have added to their playlist? Then listen here!
A great mix of new music, forgotten classics and your requests with Amy Lamé.
Wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Legendary DJ and broadcaster and selector David Rodigan, who's also a passionate champion of reggae music, joins Lauren for a chat and pick of his favourite tunes.
Chris shares more of your Names In Songs, as well as giving YOU the chance to pick the song we end the show with.
Bonnie Prince Billy and John Robins join Huw to talk candidly about their respective relationships with music and alcohol.
Super producer and Bleachers frontman Jack Antonoff is in session from Maida Vale, ahead of the release of Bleachers' new album 'everyone for ten minutes'.
Gideon Coe plays his music loves from every era and genre.
3/4. Kim Gordon shares some of her favourites songs, and some of the artists that inspired Sonic Youth. Tonight's show includes music from PJ Harvey and Aimee Mann.
Singer and musician Jane Weaver presents her alternative Songs of Praise, tracks of dedication and devotion, mantra and meditation.
Mark Lamarr presents the best music you never knew you loved from the last 70 years, alongside the finest live sessions. This week Mark is joined by the Curst Sons.
Marshall Doktors are in concert from 1981, and live BBC sessions by The War On Drugs, 35 Summers, and Adam Green & Binki Shapiro.
Soundtrack the early hours with back-to-back music, plus exclusive BBC sessions.
Amy Lamé fires up Thursday with the biggest tunes and future classics, plus another pick from this week's 5Before6 DJ!
Nick chats to actor Saffron Hocking about her favourite music and her new film, Fuze.
Lauren gives you the finest blend of fantastic music for your weekday morning.
Ahead of her upcoming headline tour, singer-songwriter Brooke Combe joins Chris in the studio to chat about what fans can expect.
Huw has handpicked music, old and new, plus we round up the gigs, shows and festivals that everybody is talking about in today's Gig Lisztomania.
Reviewing this week's new releases.
Nathan and Deb are joined by Robyn superfan Lynks to celebrate the release of her new album 'Sexistential', listening to the album in full and sharing their thoughts.
Gideon Coe plays his music loves from every era and genre.
4/4. Kim Gordon picks some of her favourite songs, and also some artists that inspired Sonic Youth. This episode includes music from Nina Simone and Mazzy Star.
